mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.3% as of August 2013 showing downtrend from 2012 peaks
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal strengthening residential construction demand and forward-looking economic optimism, which increases multiplier effects through employment and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 900 thousand units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 891.0 thousand units in August 2013, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following HOUST > 900 crossings are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5%, t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero combined with sustained low inflation signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity risk premium and supports valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% and CPIAUCSL at 233.526 as of August 2013
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with subdued inflation
failure_condition: Joint condition (FEDFUNDS < 0.25% AND low inflation) fails to predict positive returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.06 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate capital investment, which translates to earnings growth and positive equity returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.3579 in August 2013, approaching pre-crisis levels
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX 5-day returns show no correlation with INDPRO month-over-month changes (correlation < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth combined with rising payroll employment signals strengthening consumer purchasing power and demand momentum, which drives corporate revenue and profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.15%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 426563.0 million and PAYEMS at 136133.0 thousand in August 2013
failure_condition: Joint condition (strong retail sales AND strong payroll growth) fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.07 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth exceeding 200k monthly net additions signals tight labor market and wage pressure, which reduces corporate profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as market reprices earnings downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136133.0 thousand in August 2013 with recent acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in < 45% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 0.20%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation measured by CPI combined with elevated unemployment signals stagflationary pressure that increases equity risk premium and reduces real cash flow valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.3% and CPIAUCSL at 233.526 in August 2013
regime_hint: stagflationary regime with conflicting macro signals
failure_condition: Stagflation condition (high inflation AND high unemployment) fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth deceleration below 2% annualized signals weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as market reprices growth downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15679.7 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2013
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in < 48% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
